items.headline: This Date in Baseball - Miguel Cabrera of the Tigers becomes the 33rd member of the 3,000 hit club
items.description:
April 23
1903 — The New York Highlanders won their first game as a major league team, 7-2 over the Washington Senators.

items.headline: Bellingham says he 'owes' Birmingham after buying cricket stake
items.description: Real Madrid star Jude Bellingham said a debt of gratitude towards his home city of Birmingham was behind his decision to buy a minority stake...

items.headline: After Fernando Mendoza at No. 1, the NFL draft’s QB board could go quiet fast
items.description:
Don't expect a run of quarterbacks to go off the board in the first round of this year's NFL draft like what happened in 2021 and 2024.
There figures to be a long gap between Fernando Mendoza's coronation as the expected No. 1 pick by the Las Vegas Raiders and when the second quarterback on most public big boards, Ty Simpson, is expected to go.

items.headline: Real Madrid's Arda Güler: Assists Tuesday
items.description: Guler assisted once to go with one shot (zero on goal), two crosses (two accurate) and three corners in Tuesday's 2-1 win over Deportivo Alaves.

items.headline: Rays' Ben Williamson: Fading into platoon role
items.description: Williamson will start at second base and bat sixth in Wednesday's game against the Reds, Ryan Bass of Rays.tv reports.

items.headline: Athletics' Jeff McNeil: Blasts first homer Tuesday
items.description: McNeil went 2-for-5 with a solo home run in Tuesday's 5-2 win over the Mariners.

items.headline: Rays' Richie Palacios: Idle against lefty
items.description: Palacios is out of the lineup for Wednesday's game against the Reds, Ryan Bass of Rays.tv reports.

items.headline: Newcastle United's Tino Livramento: Set for second scan
items.description: Livramento (thigh) is set for a secondary scan and could potentially be an option for the final two games of the season, according to Craig Hope of the Daily Mail.
